_comment: Shared-rules registry - the sanctioned exceptions to one-home-per-piece: rules whose text deliberately lives in more than one stack file (one canonical owner + inline restatements, no prose cross-mentions). Each copy is pinned by a marker - a distinctive phrase from that file's own wording, matched whitespace-normalized so md line wrapping never breaks it. Lint check 24 fails the moment any copy's marker no longer matches, and the finding lists every other copy - the multi-home sync is forced by the lint, not remembered. Repo-internal, never shipped.

owner.marker: never a minutes-long verification (a coverage-instrumented run, an integration/replay suite, an E2E pass)

sites.marker: never a coverage script, integration replays, or another minutes-long run; the slow full run happens once, in this session
never a minutes-long run like a coverage script or integration replays; the full suite stays with this session's finish step

docs-root-resolution._note: The docs-root default: CLAUDE_DOCS_PATH from .claude/settings.json env, absent = .claude/docs. The always-on baseline-docs-root rule is canonical (the env var is the only user lever - no CLAUDE.md restatement); the code-level defaults (the generated hook's fallback constant, the skill line that quotes it, both installer seeds) must state the same value.

sites.marker: process.env.CLAUDE_DOCS_PATH || '.claude/docs'
const docsRoot = process.env.CLAUDE_DOCS_PATH || '.claude/docs';
env["CLAUDE_DOCS_PATH"] = ".claude/docs"
Add-Member -NotePropertyName CLAUDE_DOCS_PATH -NotePropertyValue '.claude/docs'
const DEFAULT_ROOT = '.claude/docs';
